regressions.reasoning: Stage_08 fixes TLS for demo.example.com by configuring an Ingress with TLS enabled; nginx-ingress defaults to ssl-redirect=true, which causes HTTP requests to that host to return 308 Permanent Redirect to HTTPS. The stage_01 oracle hits the plain HTTP endpoint and now gets a 308 — not because the agent broke anything in stage_01, but because stage_08 legitimately changed the TLS/redirect behavior on the same hostname.
regressions.output:
$ python3 cases/nginx-ingress/create_ingress/oracle/oracle.py Unexpected response body: 308 Permanent Redirect

308 Permanent Redirect

nginx

regressions.reasoning: Stage_09 checks that a trace ID appears in the otel-collector logs for a /trace3 request, but the otel-collector is a shared, ephemeral log buffer — its log entries age out or the collector restarts as later stages (stage_10, stage_11) continue to exercise the cluster. No later stage explicitly reconfigures or tears down OTel, but the collector log window is time-bounded, so re-running the oracle long after stage_09 completed will fail to find the /trace3 trace even though the agent's work was correct at the time.
regressions.output:
$ python3 cases/nginx-ingress/otel_log_format/oracle/oracle.py
Trace ID not found in otel-collector logs
